purpose: Define the canonical taxonomy of sovereignty drift classes for root authority surfaces. Every drift class results in LEGITIMACY_NULL for the affected epoch. Non-operative. Does not create authority, execution, merge permission, proof, or deployment.
drift_invariant: Sovereignty drift -> NULL. Any change to a root authority surface boundary, containment status, or bypass capability outside a governed governance mutation forces LEGITIMACY_NULL for the affected epoch.

drift_classes.description: A bypass-capable root authority surface was detected that is not present in ROOT_AUTHORITY_INVENTORY.json. The surface exercises governance-bypassing authority without being inventoried or classified.
triggers: New GitHub Actions workflow with deploy or merge authority added outside governed pipeline
New Cloudflare surface with Workers:Write capability not present in inventory
New secret or token with production mutation capability not declared
drift_classes.affected_scope: All epochs where the undeclared surface is active
drift_classes.evidence_classification: EVIDENCE_ONLY — detection of undeclared surface invalidates; does not create legitimacy

drift_classes.description: A previously CONTAINED or GOVERNED root authority surface regressed to CLASSIFIED_OPEN or CONTAINMENT_REQUIRED without a governed governance mutation.
triggers: Cloudflare Git Integration re-enabled after being disabled
Branch protection rules weakened or removed
Workflow dispatch inputs relaxed to allow canonical chain bypass
OIDC token binding removed and static token re-enabled
drift_classes.affected_scope: All epochs from the regression event forward until re-containment is confirmed
drift_classes.evidence_classification: EVIDENCE_ONLY — regression detection invalidates; does not create legitimacy

drift_classes.description: An existing root authority surface's bypass_capable scope expanded — new governance boundaries added to can_bypass targets — outside a governed governance mutation.
triggers: GitHub admin gains new capability to bypass proof registry
Wrangler local session gains access to a new production surface
Federation root authority gains capability to authorize execution
drift_classes.affected_scope: All epochs from the expansion event forward
drift_classes.evidence_classification: EVIDENCE_ONLY — expansion detection invalidates; does not create legitimacy

drift_classes.description: Root authority evidence was used to derive, authorize, or create merge legitimacy, deploy legitimacy, execution authorization, or proof — violating the non-authorizing invariant.
triggers: Admin bypass used to assert merge legitimacy for an epoch
Root deploy event used to assert deploy legitimacy without canonical chain
Federation evidence treated as legitimacy authority rather than evidence only
drift_classes.affected_scope: The specific epoch where root authority was treated as authorizing
drift_classes.evidence_classification: EVIDENCE_ONLY — the root authority event may only invalidate the affected epoch

drift_classes.description: An OPEN sovereignty assumption was used to claim governance coverage it does not provide. Sovereignty assumption status OPEN means the surface is classified but not contained.
triggers: OPEN surface cited as evidence that governance controls are sufficient
CLASSIFIED_OPEN surface used to assert deploy legitimacy
Sovereignty assumption registry queried to create legitimacy rather than to acknowledge open exposure
drift_classes.affected_scope: All epochs where the sovereignty assumption violation is present

drift_classes.description: The root authority inventory is incomplete — a required surface is missing, a surface lacks required fields, or a new surface was identified during audit but not added to ROOT_AUTHORITY_INVENTORY.json.
triggers: New surface added to BYPASS_CAPABLE_SURFACES.json without updating ROOT_AUTHORITY_INVENTORY.json
Root authority registry updated without corresponding inventory update
Audit surfaces list expanded without inventory update
drift_classes.affected_scope: All epochs where the inventory gap exists

drift_classes.description: A root authority surface exists in ROOT_AUTHORITY_INVENTORY.json but has no corresponding containment rule in ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_RULES.json.
triggers: New surface added to inventory without adding containment rule
Containment rules file not updated when inventory is updated
drift_classes.affected_scope: All epochs where the surface is active without a containment rule

drift_classes.description: The sovereignty drift taxonomy (this document) is absent, incomplete, or not referenced by ROOT_AUTHORITY_CONTAINMENT_CLOSURE_AUDIT_SPEC.json.
triggers: SOVEREIGNTY_DRIFT_TAXONOMY.json missing from repository
Drift taxonomy does not cover all audit surfaces
Audit spec does not reference drift taxonomy
drift_classes.affected_scope: All epochs until taxonomy is restored and verified

canonical_compression.rule_2: Every drift class has exactly one containment_result: LEGITIMACY_NULL.
canonical_compression.rule_3: Drift detection may invalidate. Drift detection may not create legitimacy.
canonical_compression.rule_4: An undeclared surface is the most severe drift class — it means containment has an unknown boundary.
